name: Serra da Nogueira and Azibo Reservoir Loop
name_original: Subida ao Santuário de Nossa Senhora da Serra — circular desde o Praia Albufeira do Azibo
description: This difficult 42.0-mile road cycling route from Praia Albufeira do Azibo climbs 4360 feet to Nossa Senhora da Serra Sanctuary with panorami
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Santa Combinha navigate a landscape characterized by varied terrain, including reservoir areas and significant elevation changes. The region features a mix of rural roads and routes that ascend into hilly areas, offering challenging climbs. Road cyclists can expect to encounter diverse natural settings, from open landscapes to routes near water bodies. The area provides a network of paths suitable for those seeking moderate to difficult road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Santa Combinha The most popular road cycling route is Macedo de Cavaleiros e Albufeira do Azibo — circular o Paisagem do Morais, a 25.4 miles (40.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 5 minutes to complete. This route offers scenic views of the Morais landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is From Macedo de Cavaleiros to the Albufeira do Azibo beach — circular, a difficult 43.5 miles (69.9 km) path. This route features significant climbs and views towards the Albufeira do Azibo beach. Local road cyclists also love the Serra da Nogueira and Azibo Reservoir Loop, a 42.0 miles (67.6 km) trail leading through the Serra da Nogueira and around the Azibo Reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 51 minutes. Road cycling around Santa Combinha is defined by hilly terrain, reservoir areas, and rural landscapes. The network offers options for moderate and difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 130 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Santa Combinha's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Santa Combinha?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes around Santa Combinha, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. These routes are primarily categorized as moderate to difficult, catering to riders looking for a challenge.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level for road cycling routes in Santa Combinha?
A: Road cycling in Santa Combinha is characterized by moderate to difficult routes, often featuring significant elevation changes. There are 12 moderate routes and 14 difficult routes, making the area well-suited for experienced cyclists seeking challenging climbs and varied terrain.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Santa Combinha?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Santa Combinha are designed as circular loops. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1401104092/macedo-de-cavaleiros-e-albufeira-do-azibo-circular-o-paisagem-do-morais" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Macedo de Cavaleiros e Albufeira do Azibo — circular o Paisagem do Morais&lt;/a&gt; and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1401105172/from-macedo-de-cavaleiros-to-the-albufeira-do-azibo-beach-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Macedo de Cavaleiros to the Albufeira do Azibo beach — circular&lt;/a&gt; are both circular options. Another notable loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1401577350/serra-da-nogueira-and-azibo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra da Nogueira and Azibo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while road cycling in Santa Combinha?
A: The region offers diverse natural settings, including reservoir areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4886151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufeira do Azibo&lt;/a&gt;, hilly terrain, and rural roads. You'll encounter open landscapes, views of the Morais landscape, and routes that ascend into the Serra da Nogueira.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes with scenic viewpoints in Santa Combinha?
A: Absolutely. Many routes offer scenic vistas, particularly those that traverse higher elevations or pass by water bodies. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1401104092/macedo-de-cavaleiros-e-albufeira-do-azibo-circular-o-paisagem-do-morais" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Macedo de Cavaleiros e Albufeira do Azibo — circular o Paisagem do Morais&lt;/a&gt; route provides scenic views of the Morais landscape, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1401105172/from-macedo-de-cavaleiros-to-the-albufeira-do-azibo-beach-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Macedo de Cavaleiros to the Albufeira do Azibo beach — circular&lt;/a&gt; offers views towards the Albufeira do Azibo beach.

Q: What are some of the longer road cycling routes in the area?
A: For those seeking longer rides, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32144269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Comba de Rossas – View of Pinela loop from Podence e Santa Combinha&lt;/a&gt; covers approximately 83 km, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32142364" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Road to Nogueira – Santa Comba de Rossas loop from Podence e Santa Combinha&lt;/a&gt; is around 80 km, both featuring significant elevation gain.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Santa Combinha?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the challenging hilly terrain, the scenic reservoir areas, and the quiet rural roads that define the region's cycling experience.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or landmarks near the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, several natural attractions are close to the cycling routes. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6445599" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Geopark of Macedo de Cavaleiros&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4886151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufeira do Azibo&lt;/a&gt;, and its associated &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6342785" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufeira do Azibo Beach&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4947769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeira River Beach&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable spot.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Santa Combinha?
A: While not explicitly stated in the route data, regions with varied terrain and reservoirs like Santa Combinha typically offer pleasant cycling conditions in spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, especially on routes with less shade, while winter might bring cooler, wetter weather.

Q: Are there any shorter, moderate road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, for a moderate experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1401104092/macedo-de-cavaleiros-e-albufeira-do-azibo-circular-o-paisagem-do-morais" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Macedo de Cavaleiros e Albufeira do Azibo — circular o Paisagem do Morais&lt;/a&gt; route is a good option. It's about 40.9 km long and takes approximately 2 hours and 5 minutes to complete, offering scenic views without the most extreme climbs.

Q: What should I know about elevation changes on Santa Combinha's road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling in Santa Combinha involves significant elevation changes. Routes often feature challenging climbs, with some difficult routes accumulating over 1,300 meters of ascent, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1401577350/serra-da-nogueira-and-azibo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra da Nogueira and Azibo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;. Be prepared for hilly terrain.
tips: Small town located a few kilometers from Bragança, in a land of hills and small towns in round mountains!
Nice climb and road that takes us to what is called the Santuário de Nossa Senhora da Serra, with beautiful views of the surrounding hills.

Praia Fluvial da Fraga da Pegada is a river beach located in the Albufeira do Azibo, near Macedo de Cavaleiros, in the Trás-os-Montes region (northern Portugal). It is one of the most highly regarded destinations for nature tourism and water sports in the area.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This challenging route usually takes an average of 3 hours and 52 minutes to complete. However, your actual time may vary depending on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: No, this route is consistently rated as difficult and is best suited for experienced road cyclists. It involves a substantial elevation gain of approximately 1,330 meters, making it a strenuous workout.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features mostly well-paved surfaces that are easy to ride, making it suitable for road bikes. However, be prepared for significant and demanding ascents, especially on the climb to the Santuário de Nossa Senhora da Serra.

faqs.question: Where does the route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts from Praia Albufeira do Azibo. Parking is generally available in the vicinity of the beach, which serves as a popular starting point for activities in the area.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and points of interest can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse and picturesque scenery, starting from the beautiful Praia Albufeira do Azibo, which is part of a Protected Landscape and the Meseta Ibérica Transfrontier Biosphere Reserve. The route offers opportunities to observe various wildlife and flora, including cork oaks and a variety of birds. As you ascend, you'll be rewarded with panoramic views, especially from higher elevations like the Serra de Bornes, and the cultural focal point of the Santuário de Nossa Senhora da Serra.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route can be enjoyed during many seasons, but summer offers the chance to see paragliders and hang gliders from the Serra de Bornes. Consider spring or autumn for potentially milder temperatures, which can be more comfortable for the significant climbs.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: While the general area around Albufeira do Azibo is often dog-friendly, this is a road cycling route. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it is accustomed to riding in a trailer or can safely accompany you on a leash in areas where it's appropriate and safe, especially given the road nature of the trail.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need special permits or to pay an entrance fee to cycle this specific road route. While the route passes through the Protected Landscape of Albufeira do Azibo, general road cycling does not require specific permits.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other official cycling trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route briefly intersect with parts of the Ecopista do Tua and the Linha do Tua trails, though these make up a small percentage of the overall circular journey.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants along the route or near the start/end point?
faqs.answer: You can find facilities such as cafes and restaurants near the starting point at Praia Albufeira do Azibo. There may also be limited options in nearby settlements like Santa Comba de Rossas, and potentially at or near the Santuário de Nossa Senhora da Serra, especially during peak seasons.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the starting point at Praia Albufeira do Azibo?
faqs.answer: Public transport options to Praia Albufeira do Azibo can be limited. It is advisable to check local bus schedules from nearby towns like Macedo de Cavaleiros, as direct routes might not be frequent, especially outside of peak tourist seasons.

waypoints.name: Climb to the Santuário de Nossa Senhora da Serra
